title: Wart follow-up

red_flags.text: Supports clinician-observed morphology and distribution, diagnostic uncertainty, pain, bleeding, secondary infection, and immunosuppression context without inferring diagnosis or treatment.
red_flags.rationale: The exact diagnosis and complications section supports clinician skin inspection, distribution and relevant immune, pain, bleeding or infection context only when assessed.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-observed morphology and distribution, diagnostic uncertainty, pain, bleeding, secondary infection, and immunosuppression context without inferring diagnosis or treatment.

source.title: British Association of Dermatologists' guidelines for the management of cutaneous warts 2014

follow_up.text: Supports documenting wart identity, age, lesion site, duration, persistence, multiplicity, immune context, pain, function, and cosmetic impact.
follow_up.rationale: The exact wart terminology and natural-history section supports documenting wart follow-up, duration, interval change, pain or functional impact, symptoms, site and persistence without inferring response or resolution.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports documenting wart identity, age, lesion site, duration, persistence, multiplicity, immune context, pain, function, and cosmetic impact.

source_limitations: Exact lesion site, count and dimensions, morphology, genital exclusion, immune and vascular context, previous treatment and response, diagnosis, procedure, medicine, referral, and follow-up timing remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
